thinking: Step 1: REQ-ARCH-0100 mentions SWC_SensorFusion — this is a top-level ApplicationSWC responsible for fusing sensor data.
Step 2: REQ-ARCH-0101 describes a cyclic runnable RE_FuseData with 10ms trigger for sensor data fusion processing.
Step 3: REQ-ARCH-0102 specifies that SWC_SensorFusion shall provide fused sensor data via a Sender-Receiver interface containing FusedEnvironmentModel.
Step 4: REQ-ARCH-0103 mandates that SWC_SensorFusion detects sensor communication failures and transitions to a degraded operation mode within 50ms.
Step 5: REQ-ARCH-0104 specifies lifecycle management states for SWC_SensorFusion: Init, Running, Degraded, and Shutdown.
Step 6: REQ-ARCH-0200 mentions SWC_VehicleControl — this component computes vehicle control commands based on fused sensor data.
Step 7: REQ-ARCH-0201 specifies that SWC_VehicleControl receives fused environment model data via R-Port with a Sender-Receiver interface.
Step 8: REQ-ARCH-0202 describes a cyclic runnable in SWC_VehicleControl to compute steering and braking commands every 20ms.
Step 9: REQ-ARCH-0203 specifies that SWC_VehicleControl provides vehicle control commands to SWC_ActuatorManager via Sender-Receiver interface.
Step 10: REQ-ARCH-0300 mentions SWC_SafetyMonitor — this component continuously monitors system health and can trigger emergency stop.
Step 11: REQ-ARCH-0301 specifies that SWC_SafetyMonitor receives sensor fusion status and vehicle control status via R-Ports.
Step 12: REQ-ARCH-0302 mandates a Client-Server interface for diagnostic DTC read requests from SWC_Diagnostics.
Step 13: REQ-ARCH-0303 specifies that SWC_SafetyMonitor triggers safe state within 20ms if any ASIL-D violation is detected.
Step 14: REQ-ARCH-0400 mentions SWC_ActuatorManager — this component translates control commands to actuator-specific signals.
Step 15: REQ-ARCH-0401 specifies that SWC_ActuatorManager receives control commands from SWC_VehicleControl and provides actuator signals via Sender-Receiver interfaces.
architecture_summary: The ADAS system consists of several ApplicationSWCs (SensorFusion, VehicleControl, SafetyMonitor, ActuatorManager) interacting through various R-Ports and S-Ports. Each component has specific responsibilities such as sensor data fusion, vehicle control command computation, safety monitoring, and actuator signal translation.

elaborated.logic_flow: The runnable RE_FuseData processes sensor data from multiple sources and fuses them into a unified environment model.

diagram_hints.activity: Sensor data reception, fusion logic, and environment model output.

elaborated.logic_flow: The runnable RE_FuseData processes sensor data from multiple sources and fuses them into a unified environment model.

diagram_hints.activity: Sensor data reception, fusion logic, and environment model output.

elaborated.logic_flow: The SWC_SensorFusion provides the fused environment model data to consuming SWCs via a Sender-Receiver interface.
The SWC_SensorFusion detects sensor communication failures and transitions to a degraded operation mode within 50ms.
The SWC_SensorFusion implements lifecycle management with Init, Running, Degraded, and Shutdown states.
The runnable RE_ComputeCommands computes vehicle control commands based on fused sensor data.

diagram_hints.activity: Fused environment model reception, command computation, and output.

elaborated.logic_flow: The SWC_VehicleControl receives fused environment model data via R-Port with a Sender-Receiver interface.
The SWC_VehicleControl provides vehicle control commands to SWC_ActuatorManager via Sender-Receiver interface.
The SWC_ActuatorManager receives control commands from SWC_VehicleControl and provides actuator signals via Sender-Receiver interfaces.
The runnable RE_MonitorSystem continuously monitors system health and can trigger emergency stop.

diagram_hints.activity: System health monitoring and emergency stop logic.

elaborated.logic_flow: The SWC_SafetyMonitor receives sensor fusion status and vehicle control status via R-Ports.
The SWC_SafetyMonitor provides a Client-Server interface for diagnostic DTC read requests from SWC_Diagnostics.
The SWC_SafetyMonitor detects critical failures and triggers emergency stop within 50ms.
The runnable RE_TranslateCommands translates vehicle control commands into actuator signals.

diagram_hints.activity: Control command reception, signal translation, and output.

elaborated.logic_flow: The SWC_ActuatorManager receives control commands from SWC_VehicleControl and provides actuator signals via Sender-Receiver interfaces.
The SWC_ActuatorManager provides actuator signals to the physical actuators.
